text: Happy Valentines Day everyone. Treat yourself to something nice today. I got myself flowers from HEB and I regret nothing.
Tried a boxing class at the YMCA today. I am sore everywhere but I feel great. Never too old to try something new.
Does anyone have a good recipe for chicken tortilla soup? Mine never comes out like my mothers.
Beautiful service at Grace Community this morning. Grateful for this church family.
Luis called tonight from Houston. He is doing well at work. Proud of that boy even when he forgets to call his mother on time.
The bluebonnets on the highway are gorgeous this year. Spring in Texas is still the prettiest thing.
April is Parkinsons Awareness Month. 1 million Americans live with it including me. Be kind to people who move a little slower. We are doing our best.
My sister Carmen came over today and helped me organize the house. I do not deserve her but I am keeping her.
Happy Cinco de Mayo! Made my mothers rice this morning. The kitchen smelled right for the first time in a long time.
Happy Mothers Day to all the moms. My Luis called me from Houston first thing this morning. That boy still makes me smile.
Church potluck was wonderful yesterday. I brought store bought cookies and nobody said a word. Love this community.
Boxing class group photo! I am in the second row with the red gloves. These people are the highlight of my week.
Happy Fathers Day to the dads. Thinking of my father today. He would have been 89.
Luis visited from Houston this weekend. Two days of old movies and home cooking. My heart is full.
Happy 4th of July! Staying in today. The fireworks are too loud for me anymore. Enjoying the quiet with a glass of lemonade.
108 degrees in Austin today. I am not going outside until October.
Recommendation needed: does anyone know a good handyman in South Austin? I have a leaky faucet that is driving me crazy.
Started watching that show everyone talks about on Netflix. I fell asleep by episode 2. Maybe I am just not a TV person anymore.
Six months of boxing class at the YMCA. My arms are stronger than they have been in years. You are never too old to start.
Farmers market with Carmen today. Came home with way too many tomatoes. If anyone wants some come by before they go bad.
Finally some cooler weather in Austin. I can walk to the mailbox without melting. The little things.
Good appointment with my doctor today. Grateful for a care team that actually listens.
Happy Halloween! I left candy on the porch because I cannot get to the door fast enough for the trick or treaters. They took the whole bowl in 10 minutes. Kids are fast.
Thank you to all who served. Thinking of my father today. Happy Veterans Day.
Thankful for Carmen. Thankful for Luis. Thankful for my church and my neighbors and the pharmacist who always remembers my name. It takes a village.
Put up my little Christmas tree today. It is crooked and the lights are uneven. It is perfect.
Luis is here for Christmas! He brought tamales from Tia Rosa. The apartment smells like home.
Merry Christmas to everyone. Wishing you peace and people who love you.
Happy New Year everyone. My resolution is simple this year. Keep going.
Made arroz con pollo from my mothers recipe last night. It tasted right. I ate it slowly and thought about her kitchen in San Antonio.
Three new people at boxing class today. One of them was nervous. I told her it gets easier. I hope I was right.
One year of boxing class. I can do 15 more reps than when I started. My right hook is no joke.
Happy Valentines Day. Carmen sent me a card. Luis sent hearts. I made myself a nice dinner. That is more than enough.
Good sermon today. The woman next to me held my hand during the prayer. Small kindnesses are everything.
Who else is tired of getting robocalls? I got four today. Four! Leave me alone I am not buying anything.
The mockingbird is back on my porch rail. Every morning with the coffee. I think we have an understanding.
Bluebonnets are starting on 290. Has anyone been to the Brenham fields yet this year?
Good conversation at support group today. Met some really strong people. Sometimes just being in a room with others who understand is the whole point.
Quiet Sunday morning on the porch. Coffee and the mockingbird. No phone. No news. Just this.
Coach introduced a balance beam exercise at boxing today. I made it all the way across without falling. Nobody was more surprised than me.
Palm Sunday at Grace Community. The children's choir sang and I did not cry. Ok I cried a little.
April is Parkinsons Awareness Month. A year ago I was scared. Now I am still scared sometimes but also stronger. If you are newly diagnosed know that it gets easier to carry.
Happy birthday Carmen!! The best sister anyone could ask for. I love you more than I say.
Anyone know a good place for breakfast tacos near South Lamar? I am tired of my own cooking.
Carmen came over to help with paperwork today. Two hours at the kitchen table. She writes, I talk. We make a good team.
Happy Easter everyone. He is risen.
I found my mothers old recipe box in the back of the closet. Her handwriting is still the most beautiful thing I have ever seen.
Watched Jeopardy and got 12 answers right tonight. Still got it.
Someone at group today said we are not broken we are adapting. I have been thinking about that all week.
Good day today. The sun was out, the porch was warm, and I made it through everything on my list. Some days that is the whole victory.
